summary: Scanned top HN stories for new product mentions.

items.name: Plain
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Plain – The full-stack Python framework designed for humans and agents
items.summary: A full-stack Python framework forked from Django, optimized for AI agent readability and review.
items.why_included: An opinionated framework explicitly targeting the agentic era, creating a focused discussion on agent-framework utility.
items.evidence: Commenters debated its necessity as a new framework versus just using standard Django/Python ecosystems that agents are already trained on.

items.name: LangAlpha
items.source_story_title: Show HN: LangAlpha – what if Claude Code was built for Wall Street?
items.summary: An open-source agent harness designed for persistent, domain-aware investment research.
items.why_included: Addresses core limitations of current agent harnesses like context bloat and session persistence for complex research.
items.evidence: Users appreciated the persistent sandbox approach for iterative research but raised significant regulatory/compliance auditability concerns.

items.name: Jujutsu (jj)
items.summary: A version control CLI using Git as a backend with a mutable working-copy mental model.
items.why_included: Offers a distinct workflow that simplifies history rewriting and commit management compared to traditional Git.
items.evidence: Commenters highlighted the 'jj undo' feature as a major safety improvement and praised the anonymous branching workflow.
